item.title: Why is SIS Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: On 18-May, SIS Ltd's stock price rose by 1.23% to ₹394.30, continuing a four-day winning streak that has seen the share gain 5.59%. This upward momentum is supported by robust quarterly financial performance and sustained outperformance relative to the broader market and sector benchmarks. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is Elcid Investments Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: As of 18-May, Elcid Investments Ltd’s stock price has fallen by 2.5% to ₹1,17,000, reflecting a broader trend of underperformance relative to market benchmarks and persistent concerns over its fundamental strength. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is NCC Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: On 18-May, NCC Ltd’s stock price fell sharply by 4.56% to close at ₹152.65, continuing a downward trend that has seen the share price underperform both its sector and broader market indices over recent weeks and months. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is Rajapalayam Mills Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: On 18 May, Rajapalayam Mills Ltd witnessed a notable decline in its share price, falling by 2.23% to close at ₹776.00. This drop reflects a continuation of recent negative momentum, with the stock underperforming both its sector and the broader market benchmarks. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is Him Teknoforge Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: On 18 May, Him Teknoforge Ltd witnessed a significant decline in its share price, closing at ₹201.20, down ₹9.30 or 4.42% from the previous close. This drop reflects a continuation of recent negative momentum and subdued investor participation, despite the company’s strong long-term performance relative to the broader market. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is Hinduja Global Solutions Ltd falling/rising?
item.description: As of 18-May, Hinduja Global Solutions Ltd (HGS) shares have declined to ₹401.50, down 1.21% on the day, continuing a downward trend driven by persistent negative financial results and underperformance relative to market benchmarks. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Why is The Jammu & Kashmir Bank Ltd. falling/rising?
item.description: On 18-May, The Jammu & Kashmir Bank Ltd. witnessed a notable decline in its share price, falling by 2.52% to close at ₹127.40. This drop comes amid a short-term correction despite the bank’s robust long-term performance and strong fundamental indicators. Time: 1 hour ago
